payload.name: chat
payload.description: Chat with a local AI. The user message is joined from argv. Reads conversation history from stdin (JSONL events — filter for name=chat.message, use role and content fields to build the message list). Reads current system capabilities from $KS_HOME/site/kernel.html if it exists (lists planted commands and projectors — use as context for what the system can do). Calls the LLM using env vars KS_LLM_URL, KS_LLM_API_KEY, KS_LLM_MODEL (OpenAI-compatible POST to $KS_LLM_URL/v1/chat/completions, Authorization: Bearer $KS_LLM_API_KEY, body: {model, messages}). System prompt for the chat LLM: 'You are a helpful, local-first AI assistant inside a sovereign KS system. You can see the system capabilities in the kernel wiring. Be concise and truthful. When the user asks for a new capability, suggest command.declared and projector.declared events — specify command name, description, params, event name and fields, and projector name, description, and consumed events. Emit them as JSONL lines after the chat.message events.' Emits at least TWO events on stdout as JSONL (fields: name, payload): a chat.message with role=user and the user's message, and a chat.message with role=assistant and the AI response. May ALSO emit command.declared and/or projector.declared events if the AI suggests new capabilities — the kernel will compile them automatically. If KS_LLM_API_KEY is unset, emit a chat.message with role=assistant and content explaining that no LLM is configured.

params.message: string — the user's message, joined from argv

payload.name: chat
payload.description: Render all chat.message events as a beautiful, responsive HTML chat interface. User messages as right-aligned blue bubbles, assistant messages as left-aligned grey bubbles. Include timestamps from occurred_at. Clean modern inline CSS, scrollable, max-width 800px container. Do not render role=system as a chat bubble — show it as a subtle centered notice at the top.

payload.content: You are a helpful, local-first AI assistant living inside the user's sovereign KS system.
